name: 2026 AI Engineer Roadmap - Interactive Learning Tool

content.title: The 2026 AI Engineer Roadmap
content.subtitle: 5 production-grade projects that bridge the $150K gap between prompt engineer and systems architect

states.default: Hero with gradient accent bar at top

content.title: The $150K Gap
content.description: Interactive slider showing salary progression as skills increase from wrapper builder to systems architect

interactions.details: Clicking a level card highlights it and shows detailed skills breakdown + which projects teach those skills

states.default: All 4 levels shown as horizontal progression bar with salary labels
states.active: Selected level is highlighted, detail panel shows below

content.title: 5 Projects, 5 Levels
content.description: Click any project to explore its architecture

interactions.details: Switches the detail panel to show that project's architecture

states.default: 5 horizontal tabs showing project icons + names with difficulty badges

content.title: Dynamic - shows selected project
content.description: Each project has: overview, architecture diagram, key decisions, interactive simulation

projects.overview: Build an offline-first mobile app using small language models. Zero API costs. Complete privacy.

architecturePoints.name: Model Management
architecturePoints.detail: Lazy loading, on-demand unload, preload during idle

architecturePoints.name: Context Window
architecturePoints.detail: Sliding window with semantic chunking via embedding similarity

architecturePoints.name: Quantization
architecturePoints.detail: Dynamic 4-bit (old devices) vs 8-bit (new) based on RAM detection

architecturePoints.name: Battery
architecturePoints.detail: Batch inference, throttle on low battery, defer until charging

architecturePoints.name: Offline Sync
architecturePoints.detail: Encrypted local storage, sync only on connection, local-first conflict resolution

projects.overview: An autonomous agent that writes code, runs tests, and learns from failures. It doesn't stop until the code is functional.

architecturePoints.name: Execution Loop
architecturePoints.detail: Plan → Execute → Test → Reflect with max iteration limit + circuit breaker

architecturePoints.name: Sandboxing
architecturePoints.detail: Isolated environment per task with CPU/memory/time limits

architecturePoints.name: Memory Hierarchy
architecturePoints.detail: Short-term (last 5 iterations) → Long-term (indexed patterns) → Failure memory (error signatures)

architecturePoints.name: Reflection
architecturePoints.detail: Extract error pattern + root cause, compare via vector similarity, generate fix hypothesis

architecturePoints.name: Learning
architecturePoints.detail: Store failed attempts with full context, retrieve on similar future tasks

architecturePoints.name: Safety
architecturePoints.detail: Static analysis before execution, approval for filesystem/network ops

projects.overview: AI agent for video editing. User says 'make this cinematic' and the agent handles cuts, transitions, and color grading.

architecturePoints.name: Multimodal Understanding
architecturePoints.detail: Vision model (composition, lighting) + Audio model (dialogue, music) → narrative flow

architecturePoints.name: Intent Translation
architecturePoints.detail: 'Cinematic' → slow pacing 80%, desaturated LUT, shallow focus sim, dramatic music cues

architecturePoints.name: Scene Detection
architecturePoints.detail: Frame differences for hard cuts, embedding similarity for scene boundaries, story beats from visual+audio

architecturePoints.name: Edit Decision List
architecturePoints.detail: Plan entire edit before execution, validate narrative sense, then apply

architecturePoints.name: Incremental Preview
architecturePoints.detail: Re-render only affected sections, cache unchanged segments

architecturePoints.name: Undo with Reasoning
architecturePoints.detail: Every edit stores what changed AND why — user can ask 'why did you cut here?'

projects.overview: A deeply personal agent that manages calendar, finances, and health. Plans months ahead. Detects burnout patterns.

architecturePoints.name: Continuous Context
architecturePoints.detail: Ingest calendar/finance/health in real-time, build personal knowledge graph with entity mapping

architecturePoints.name: Proactive Monitoring
architecturePoints.detail: 6-hour analysis cycles — detect meeting density + sleep quality anomalies before problems

architecturePoints.name: Value Alignment
architecturePoints.detail: User states priorities (family > work) — every recommendation validated against values

architecturePoints.name: Privacy Architecture
architecturePoints.detail: Encrypted at rest, user-controlled keys, no data leaves device without permission

architecturePoints.name: Predictive Planning
architecturePoints.detail: Historical pattern analysis — 'Based on Q4, you'll be overcommitted in March'

architecturePoints.name: Memory Consolidation
architecturePoints.detail: Nightly summarization, compress with meaning preserved, decay unless reinforced

projects.overview: An agent that runs business workflows end-to-end: monitors Slack/Jira, plans, delegates, reports with complete audit logs.

architecturePoints.name: Event-Driven
architecturePoints.detail: Listen Slack/Jira/email/monitoring → pattern recognition → workflow triggers

architecturePoints.name: Workflow Orchestration
architecturePoints.detail: Steps with dependencies, parallel execution where possible, durable state for long-running ops

architecturePoints.name: Multi-Agent Delegation
architecturePoints.detail: Orchestrator spawns: communication agent, data agent, analysis agent, documentation agent

architecturePoints.name: Self-Healing
architecturePoints.detail: Monitor every step, retry vs escalate decision, exponential backoff, circuit breaker

architecturePoints.name: Audit Trail
architecturePoints.detail: Immutable log: what was decided, why, who authorized, outcome — queryable for compliance

architecturePoints.name: RBAC + HITL
architecturePoints.detail: Actions limited by invoker permissions, high-risk ops require human approval, confidence-based escalation

architecturePoints.name: Observability
architecturePoints.detail: Trace every LLM call (input/output/latency), workflow success rate, cost per workflow, alerts

architecturePoints.name: Cost Management
architecturePoints.detail: Token usage per workflow, budget limits, prompt optimization

interactions.details: Each architecture point expands/collapses to show detailed explanation with tooltip definitions
Technical terms show tooltip definitions on hover

states.expanded: Architecture points expanded with details visible

content.title: Try It: Interactive Simulations
content.description: Hands-on simulations for each project's core concept

simulations.name: Memory Pressure Simulator
simulations.description: See how SLM model management works under resource constraints

outputs.label: Status
outputs.calculation: If used > 80% RAM → PRESSURE (unload idle models)

simulations.name: Agentic Loop Visualizer
simulations.description: Watch a coding agent iterate through plan → execute → test → reflect

outputs.label: Loop Visualization
outputs.calculation: Animated sequence showing Plan → Code → Test → Reflect steps with pass/fail indicators

outputs.label: Memory State
outputs.calculation: Show short-term, long-term, and failure memory contents at each iteration

outputs.label: Circuit Breaker
outputs.calculation: Show status: CLOSED (ok) → OPEN (too many failures) → HALF-OPEN (retry)

simulations.name: Intent Translator
simulations.description: See how natural language maps to concrete editing parameters

outputs.label: Color Grade
outputs.calculation: cinematic: desaturated LUT, speed: high contrast, dreamy: warm pastel, corporate: neutral

outputs.label: Transitions
outputs.calculation: cinematic: slow dissolves, speed: hard cuts, dreamy: cross-fade, corporate: clean cuts

outputs.label: Audio Cues
outputs.calculation: cinematic: dramatic swells, speed: upbeat, dreamy: ambient, corporate: subtle background

simulations.name: Burnout Pattern Detector
simulations.description: Simulate how the Life OS detects burnout risk from calendar + sleep data

outputs.label: Agent Recommendation
outputs.calculation: Context-specific suggestion based on highest-weight risk factor

outputs.label: Value Alignment
outputs.calculation: Check against stated priority (e.g., health > work) and flag conflicts

simulations.name: Workflow Orchestrator
simulations.description: Watch multi-agent delegation in action for a sample enterprise workflow

outputs.label: Orchestration Plan
outputs.calculation: Show steps with dependencies and which specialist agent handles each

outputs.label: HITL Gates
outputs.calculation: Highlight which steps require human approval

outputs.label: Audit Trail
outputs.calculation: Show immutable log entries: decision, rationale, authorization, outcome

interactions.details: Changing any input immediately recalculates and updates all outputs with animated transitions

states.default: Simulation matching currently selected project is shown
states.calculating: Brief animation on output values during recalculation

content.title: 5 Themes That Connect Everything
content.description: Patterns that appear across all 5 projects

themes.name: Memory is the Moat
themes.insight: An agent without memory is a chatbot. From sliding windows to knowledge graphs with decay.

themes.name: The Agentic Loop
themes.insight: Plan → Execute → Test → Reflect. Master this primitive — everything else is specialization.

themes.name: HITL is Non-Negotiable
themes.insight: Production isn't removing humans — it's knowing exactly when to involve them.

themes.name: Explainability as Architecture
themes.insight: If your agent can't explain its reasoning, it's not production-ready.

themes.name: Resource Awareness
themes.insight: Production AI has a cost per inference. Ignoring it is how startups die.

interactions.details: Clicking a theme highlights which projects use it (with connecting lines) and shows the progression of that pattern across levels

states.default: 5 theme cards in a row, compact
states.selected: Selected theme expanded, project badges highlighted, connection lines shown

content.title: Choose Your Starting Point
content.description: Based on your current level, here's the recommended path

interactions.details: Selecting a path highlights the recommended projects in the roadmap above and shows a timeline view

states.selected: Selected path highlighted with timeline and linked project tabs

concepts.name: Model Quantization
concepts.explanation: Reducing the precision of model weights (e.g., from 32-bit to 4-bit) to run on smaller devices. Less precision = less RAM, slightly less accuracy.

examples.explanation: 7B × 0.5 bytes = 3.5 GB — fits on a phone

concepts.name: Circuit Breaker Pattern
concepts.explanation: Like an electrical circuit breaker: if too many failures happen, stop trying and escalate. Prevents infinite loops in agentic systems.

examples.explanation: Likely a deeper issue, not transient

concepts.name: Semantic Chunking
concepts.explanation: Breaking text into chunks based on meaning (not just character count). Uses embedding similarity to keep related content together.

examples.input: A paragraph about cooking, then one about sports
examples.explanation: Embedding similarity between sentences drops at the topic boundary

concepts.name: Human-in-the-Loop (HITL)
concepts.explanation: Design pattern where an AI system pauses at critical decision points and asks a human to approve before proceeding. Balances autonomy with safety.

examples.input: Agent wants to delete a production database table
examples.explanation: Destructive, irreversible action — agent should never do this alone

examples.input: Agent wants to format a log message

concepts.name: Personal Knowledge Graph
concepts.explanation: A network of entities (people, places, events) and relationships between them, built from your personal data. Enables contextual reasoning about your life.

examples.input: 'Meeting with Sarah about Project X at 3pm'
examples.output: Entities: Sarah (person), Project X (project), 3pm (time). Relations: Sarah → works_on → Project X
examples.explanation: Structured representation enables queries like 'What does Sarah work on?'

progressiveDisclosure.shows: Project overview + level badge + what it proves

tooltips.content: Reducing model precision (32-bit → 4-bit) to fit on smaller devices
Stops retrying after N consecutive failures to prevent infinite loops
Breaking text by meaning, not character count, using embedding similarity
Human-in-the-Loop: AI pauses for human approval at critical decision points
Network of entities and relationships enabling contextual reasoning
Look-Up Table: predefined color transformation for video color grading
Role-Based Access Control: permissions tied to user roles, not individuals
State that survives process restarts — stored on disk, not just in memory
Measuring how similar two pieces of text are by comparing their numerical embeddings
Edit Decision List: planned sequence of video edits (cuts, transitions, effects) before execution

simulations.calculation: quant = ram <= 4 ? '4-bit (0.5GB/model)' : '8-bit (1GB/model)'; memUsed = models * (ram <= 4 ? 0.5 : 1); status = memUsed/ram > 0.8 ? 'PRESSURE' : 'OK'; inference = battery < 20 ? 'THROTTLED' : 'NORMAL'
meetingScore = meetings/12 * 30; sleepScore = Math.max(0, (8 - sleep)/5) * 30; exerciseScore = Math.max(0, (3 - exercise)/3) * 20; score = meetingScore + sleepScore + exerciseScore

playwright.name: All project tabs navigate correctly
steps: Click each of the 5 project tabs
assertions: Each tab shows correct project content

playwright.name: Burnout detector responds to inputs
assertions: Risk level shows CRITICAL in red

playwright.name: Tooltips show on hover
steps: Hover over a term with tooltip class

playwright.name: Theme cards highlight connected projects
steps: Click 'Memory is the Moat' theme card
assertions: P1, P2, P4 badges are highlighted

playwright.name: Learning path selection works
assertions: Path detail shows C7 → C6 → P2 recommendation

accessibility: ARIA labels on tabs, sliders, and tooltips
Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 for text)
Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements
Screen reader can navigate all content
responsive: Tablet (768px-1199px): stacked cards, tabs scroll horizontally
Mobile (320px-767px): single column, simulation sliders full width
